The species Sieruela usambarica is native to Tanzania and has a unique set of characteristics that distinguish it from other species within the genus. Sieruela usambarica is further classified into the subfamily Asclepiadoideae, which contains many species of flowering plants with similar characteristics. The classification of Sieruela usambarica is based on its morphology, anatomy, and genetic features.

intro: Sieruela usambarica, also known as the Usambarea or Yellow-root, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the family Brassicaceae. Traditionally, Sieruela usambarica has been used in African folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Sieruela usambarica is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or powder, and is also used in traditional remedies such as tonics and ointments.
